reforms.description: The Polish government launched a programme aimed at reducing the administrative burden imposed on businesses by simplifying information requirements and reducing the number of documents needed for starting a business.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing administrative burdens, the programme encourages entrepreneurship and innovation, which can lead to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been launched and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly impacts the startup ecosystem by facilitating business creation.
reforms.source_quote: The government estimates the administrative burden imposed upon business by regulation at more than 5% of GDP a year and has launched a programme aiming at reducing it.

reforms.description: The implementation of new legislation on information systems in health care aims to improve the coordination and integration of information systems and medical registries, enhancing online access to integrated systems supervised by the Ministry of Health. Additionally, the 'e-health' programme aims to create a centralized source of information regarding patients' treatment history, along with a medical Internet portal accessible to all interested parties.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving health information systems can enhance healthcare delivery and efficiency, contributing to better health outcomes and economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The legislation has been implemented and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly enhances the infrastructure for health information management.
reforms.source_quote: 'e-health' aims at integrating working databases, creating both a centralised source of information regarding the history of patients’ treatment and a medical Internet portal available to all interested parties.
